role_summary: SNCC field secretary and director of voter registration; civil rights movement archivist; in this corpus's embedding his testimony clusters with James Oscar Jones, Esther M. A. Terry, Dorothy Foreman Cotton, and Charles McLaurin.
ai_reading: Long's top semantic neighbors are James Oscar Jones (entry 57, count 57), Esther M. A. Terry (entry 40), Dorothy Foreman Cotton (entry 29, SCLC Educational Director), the John Dudley joint interview (entry 64), and Charles McLaurin (entry 17). The cluster bridges SNCC field organizing and SCLC educational work, consistent with Long's documented dual role across both organizations. His +0.72 Nonviolence as Theology vs Armed Self-Defense reading places him on the Self-Defense side, joining the catalog's recurring SCLC-and-direct-action inversion pattern (Walker +0.87, McKinney +0.84, Lowery +0.78, Jones +0.67). His +0.60 Sacred Framing position fits his SCLC educational ministry; the inversion on the nonviolence axis remains the catalog's most consistent unresolved AI-vs-culture observation.
biographical_paragraph: Worth W. Long's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 131 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.

sources.title: Worth W. Long oral history
